fieldRegistry.label: Primary Reason For Home Health / Medical Necessity

fieldGroups.label: Medical Necessity And Homebound
fieldGroups.description: Narratives and evidence for home health need and homebound status.

fieldGroups.label: Diagnosis And Coding
fieldGroups.description: Diagnosis evidence, coding-sensitive rows, and escalation flags.

fieldGroups.label: Medications, Allergies, And Special Treatments
fieldGroups.description: Medication safety, allergies, injectables, and O0110 special treatments.

fieldGroups.label: Functional And Therapy Status
fieldGroups.description: GG/M functional scoring, therapy need, discipline frequency, and goals.

fieldGroups.label: Symptom And Body System Review
fieldGroups.description: Body-system review sections that QA checks for consistency and completeness.

fieldGroups.label: Risk And Safety Assessments
fieldGroups.description: Fall, wound, hospitalization, and safety-related risk tools.

fieldGroups.label: Care Plan And Coordination
fieldGroups.description: Plan-of-care orders, patient summary, skilled interventions, and care plan content.

fieldGroups.label: Supplementals And History
fieldGroups.description: Past medical history, caregiver support, living situation, immunizations, and supplementals.

fieldGroups.label: Low Priority Admin Reference
fieldGroups.description: Identity, agency, physician, and demographic reference values that should not dominate QA.

sourceHighlights.title: Homebound reason
sourceHighlights.summary: The patient is homebound due to requiring a walker, admitted for failure to thrive, AKI, hyperkalemia, and hyperglycemia. Obesity, recurrent falls, and chronic weakness with baseline limited mobility requiring a walker.

sourceHighlights.title: Medical necessity
sourceHighlights.summary: The patient requires home health services due to chronic conditions and the need for skilled interventions, including physical and occupational therapy.

sourceHighlights.title: Prior level of function
sourceHighlights.summary: The patient had extensive comorbidities and chronic weakness, with a history of chronic left-side lower extremity weakness.

sourceHighlights.title: Wound history
sourceHighlights.summary: No wound history provided in the referral documents.

sourceHighlights.title: Diet and fluid instructions
sourceHighlights.summary: No diet and fluid instructions provided in the referral documents.

sourceHighlights.title: PMH, immunizations, and DM status
sourceHighlights.summary: No PMH, immunizations, or DM status provided in the referral documents.

sourceHighlights.title: Diagnoses and possible coding support references
sourceHighlights.summary: No diagnoses or coding support references provided in the referral documents.

draftNarratives.label: Homebound Reason
draftNarratives.draft: The patient is homebound due to requiring a walker, admitted for failure to thrive, AKI, hyperkalemia, and hyperglycemia. Obesity, recurrent falls, and chronic weakness with baseline limited mobility requiring a walker.

draftNarratives.label: Medical Necessity
draftNarratives.draft: The patient requires home health services due to chronic conditions and the need for skilled interventions, including physical and occupational therapy.

draftNarratives.label: Patient Summary / Clinical Narrative
draftNarratives.draft: The patient is a 75-year-old male with a history of chronic conditions including failure to thrive, AKI, hyperkalemia, and hyperglycemia. He requires a walker for mobility due to obesity, recurrent falls, and chronic weakness. The patient is homebound and requires skilled interventions, including physical and occupational therapy.

draftNarratives.label: Skilled Interventions
draftNarratives.draft: The patient requires skilled interventions, including physical and occupational therapy, to manage chronic conditions and improve mobility.

draftNarratives.label: Plan For Next Visit
draftNarratives.draft: The plan for the next visit includes continued monitoring and treatment of underlying metabolic and infectious causes, pending PT/OT, and improvement in AKI.

draftNarratives.label: Care Plan Problems / Goals / Interventions
draftNarratives.draft: The care plan includes addressing chronic conditions, improving mobility, and managing pain and other symptoms. Goals include improving functional status and quality of life.

draftNarratives.label: Patient / Caregiver Goals
draftNarratives.draft: The patient and caregiver goals include improving mobility, managing chronic conditions, and enhancing quality of life.
